children.heading: Designation
children.label: a Designation
children.text: The facility authorized for construction under the heading “Capitol Visitor Center” under chapter 5 of title II of division B of the Omnibus Consolidated and Emergency Supplemental Appropriations Act, 1999 (; ) is designated as the Capitol Visitor Center and is a part of the Capitol.Public Law 105–277112 Stat. 2681–569
to provide enhanced security for persons working in or visiting the United States Capitol;

to improve the visitor experience by providing a structure that will afford improved visitor orientation and enhance the educational experience of those who have come to learn about the Congress and the Capitol; and
for other purposes as determined by Congress or the Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate and the Committee on House Administration of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Treatment of the Capitol Visitor Center
children.label: c Treatment of the Capitol Visitor Center

children.heading: Oversight
children.label: 1 Oversight
children.text: The Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate and the Committee on House Administration of the House of Representatives shall have oversight of the Capitol Visitor Center.

children.heading: Treatment of expansion space of the Senate and House of Representatives in the Capitol Visitor Center
children.label: 2 Treatment of expansion space of the Senate and House of Representatives in the Capitol Visitor Center

children.heading: Senate
children.label: A Senate
children.text: The expansion space of the Senate described as unassigned space under the heading “Capitol Visitor Center” under the heading “ARCHITECT OF THE CAPITOL” under title II of the Act entitled “An Act making appropriations for the Legislative Branch for the fiscal year ending , and for other purposes”, approved (; ) shall be part of the Senate wing of the Capitol.September 30, 2002November 12, 2001Public Law 107–68115 Stat. 588

children.heading: House of Representatives
children.label: B House of Representatives
children.text: The expansion space of the House of Representatives described as unassigned space under the heading “Capitol Visitor Center” under the heading “ARCHITECT OF THE CAPITOL” under title II of the Act entitled “An Act making appropriations for the Legislative Branch for the fiscal year ending , and for other purposes”, approved (; ) shall be part of the House of Representatives wing of the Capitol.September 30, 2002November 12, 2001Public Law 107–68115 Stat. 588

children.heading: Treatment of Congressional Auditorium and related adjacent areas
children.label: d Treatment of Congressional Auditorium and related adjacent areas

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate and the Committee on House Administration of the House of Representatives shall jointly prescribe regulations for the assignment of the space in the Capitol Visitor Center known as the Congressional Auditorium and the related adjacent areas.

children.heading: Related adjacent areas
children.label: 2 Related adjacent areas
children.text: The regulations under paragraph (1) shall include a designation of the areas that are related adjacent areas to the Congressional Auditorium.

children.heading: Loan agreements
children.label: A Loan agreements
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Architect of the Capitol may enter into loan agreements to place historical objects for display in the Exhibition Hall of the Capitol Visitor Center.

children.label: I
children.text: the Senate Commission on Art; and

children.label: II
children.text: the House of Representatives Fine Arts Board; and

children.label: I
children.text: the Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Committee on House Administration of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: C Effective date
children.text: This paragraph shall take effect on .December 3, 2008

children.label: i
children.text: is directly related to the purpose of the Capitol Visitor Center under subsection (b)(2);

children.label: ii
children.text: is the subject of a loan agreement entered into by the Architect of the Capitol before ; andDecember 2, 2008

children.label: iii
children.text: has been approved by the Capitol Preservation Commission; or

children.label: B
children.text: is the subject of a loan agreement described under paragraph (1)(A).

children.heading: Substitution of historical object
children.label: 4 Substitution of historical object
children.text: A loan agreement described under paragraph (3)(A)(ii) may provide for the removal of an historical object from exhibition for preservation purposes and the substitution of that object with another historical object having a comparable educational purpose.
